rfc: RFC 071 P4+ — extend N45 CSE to N64 STMT_ASSIGN index/index_set arms
predecessors: 830e44c0 (N45 — CSE for to_i64 + addr-reg sharing, never landed on main)
2a1eaed0 (N64 — STMT_ASSIGN op=index/index_set arms, landed on main)
task: Apply N45's CSE optimization (830e44c0) to N64's new STMT_ASSIGN op="index"/"index_set" arms (2a1eaed0). N64 explicitly noted N45 CSE was a follow-on. This cycle: that follow-on.
device: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 (sm_120a JIT from sm_80 PTX)

p7_reduce_sum_single_thread.note: No reduction expected — the reduce_sum kernel has only 1 IndexGet (a[to_i64(idx)]) + 1 IndexSet (out[to_i64(0)]). The IndexSet's idx is a const_int (0), NOT a Local, so the collect_shared_idx pass finds no idx Local referenced by 2+ ops. CSE for to_i64 also fires only once per kernel here (the loop-carried idx is mov-assigned to a new Local each iteration via SSA-style renaming). Honest scope: the optimization targets the multi-IndexGet element-wise shape (vec_add / vec_mul); single-IndexGet shapes are no-op.

instruction_counts_per_kernel_p5.kernel: either vec_add or vec_mul (both have identical idx-use pattern)
instruction_counts_per_kernel_p5.ptxas_registers_used_note: Unchanged; ptxas pool allocator coalesces virtual registers regardless of declared count — N45 already observed this honest scope (declared regs shrink, physical use is unchanged).

modified_funcs: _nvptx_lower_stmt — signature gains shared_idx_off param; op=index/index_set arms check _nvptx_shared_idx_has → 2-instr fast path (add only) vs 3-instr slow path (mul + add)

_nvptx_lower_func — param renamed mfn -> mfn_in; runs CSE on mfn_in -> mfn before classifier; collects shared idx locals; pushes one U64 PReg per (idx,sz) pair; inline-materialises `mul.lo.s64 %rd_idx_off_<id>_<sz>, idx_reg, sz` at first qualifying STMT_ASSIGN op=index/index_set in each block (both entry-block KERNEL loop AND main block loop)

ptx_substring_pass: vec_add: mul.lo.s64 %rd_idx_off_*, ..., 8 appears once per kernel (was 3×)
verification.cpu_codegen_unchanged: Per @F f1/f2 — only _nvptx_codegen / _nvptx_lower_func / _nvptx_lower_stmt touched; CPU codegen (codegen_arm64_darwin / codegen_x86_64_linux) untouched. nvptx_target.hexa edits ONLY fire in `_nvptx_codegen` which is filtered upstream to `gpu_kind != GPU_KIND_CPU` MFuncs.

honest_scope_g3.v2_module_loader_caveat: The hexa build path for self/main.hexa reads compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa via the v2 module loader. Without HEXA_LANG override (or cwd-relative resolution), the loader picked up the MAIN repo's nvptx_target.hexa rather than this worktree's. Verified by inspecting build/artifacts/hexa_n45_driver.c: the bare `hexa build` produced a driver with the OLD 3-arg _nvptx_lower_stmt signature (no CSE). HEXA_LANG=$WORKTREE override forced the driver to compile against the modified source. All measurements (4043 B, 12.28% delta) reflect the HEXA_LANG-built driver.
honest_scope_g3.register_ordering_delta_vs_n55_fire: The N55 multi_kernel fire (inbox/fires/rfc071_p5_multi_kernel_2026_05_21/multi_kernel.sm_80.ptx, MD5 6f7a767e...) reaches the SAME 4043 B size but different MD5 — the only delta is register-declaration ordering inside .visible .entry blocks (a permutation of `.reg .u64 %rd0..2` interleaved with other regs). Same instruction set, same kernels, identical silicon behavior. The N55 PTX was emitted from a sibling branch (worktree-agent-a21de8beece1712cd via PR-not-yet-merged) — confirms my port reaches an equivalent optimization point.
honest_scope_g3.reduce_sum_no_op: 0-byte delta on reduce_sum is the honest result. The shared_idx_off pass requires `idx_op.kind == 'local'` for membership; the IndexSet uses a const_int 0 (NOT a Local), so it never enters the shared pool. Per the task brief 'PTX size reduction may be small (<5%) — report honestly'. This is exactly that case.
honest_scope_g3.loop_iter_safety: _nvptx_collect_shared_idx_locals dedupes by (idx_local_id, elem_size). The reduce_sum back-edge loop re-binds source-level `idx` to a NEW Local id per iteration (SSA-style renaming in lower_hir), so iter N's idx Local_id never collides with iter N+1's. Verified: reduce_sum PTX is byte-identical before/after (the dedup pass found no duplicate idx Local).
honest_scope_g3.no_metal_rocm_flame_touched: Per task constraints — `git status --porcelain compiler/codegen/metal_target.hexa compiler/codegen/rocm_target.hexa stdlib/flame/` clean delta vs entry.
honest_scope_g3.no_hexa_run_used: Per feedback_no_interp_use_compiled — verification uses `hexa parse` (parse-gate) + `hexa build` (compile to native + run via /tmp/) only. No `hexa run`.

artifacts.host_p5: host_multi.c (reused from N55 fire — identical numeric reference)
artifacts.host_p7: host_reduce_sum.c (reused from N64 fire — identical reference)
